name: Nevada Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Nevada - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Nevada Division of Public and Behavioral Health — Dietitian Licensing
provider.parentOrganization: Department of Health and Human Services

address.description: 4150 Technology Way, Suite 300, Carson City, NV 89706

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (no state-mandated CE, but CDR requires 75 CPEUs per 5 years)

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

requiredDocuments.name: Fingerprint Background Check
requiredDocuments.description: Verification sent directly from the Nevada Department of Public Safety.

requiredDocuments.name: Photo ID & Proof of Citizenship
requiredDocuments.description: Valid government-issued photo identification and evidence of lawful presence.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Nevada dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license application fee is $200, plus a $37.50 fee for the fingerprint background check. The provisional license also costs $200.

mainEntity.name: When does the Nevada license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Nevada dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $200.

mainEntity.name: What are the continuing education requirements for Nevada?

acceptedAnswer.text: Nevada currently relies on the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) for continuing education tracking. There are no additional state-mandated CE hours beyond the 75 CPEUs required every five years to maintain active CDR registration.

mainEntity.name: Does Nevada have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Nevada. To work in Nevada, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Nevada?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Nevada while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
